articles.title: Australian January CPI 3.8% y/y (expected 3.7%, prior 3.8%)
articles.snippet: This is just a post for data, I'll have details and analysis posted separately. Here we go, added:Australia CPI beats estimates, lifting RBA hike odds and boosting Aussie dollarBackground:Australia January CPI preview: core inflation steady, electricity lifts headline This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.

articles.title: Fed’s Musalem: Looking forward to learning what Warsh's priorities might be
articles.snippet: St. Louis Federal Reserve (Fed) President Alberto Musalem claimed that inflation is almost a full percentage point above the Fed’s target, and the labor market is cooling in an orderly way, speaking before the Missouri Athletic Club Speaker Series on Wednesday.

articles.title: USD/ZAR: Range floor at risk as ZAR richens – Societe Generale
articles.snippet: Societe Generale analysts flag that USD/ZAR remains in a sideways consolidation but warn that failure to clear the 50‑day moving average could extend the decline toward lower projections.

articles.title: USD/CHF Price Forecast: Consolidates in bearish triangle, threatens breakdown
articles.snippet: USD/CHF extends its losses for the second consecutive trading day, down 0.14%, yet it remains trading confined to the 0.7718-0.7757 range for the last three days. At the time of writing, exchanges hands at 0.7729 above the 20-day Simple Moving Average (SMA) at 0.7726.

articles.title: XAG/USD surges above $90 as tariff fears temper and supply deficit fuels rally
articles.snippet: XAG/USD surged about 4% on Tuesday, rallying sharply to close near $91 in a session driven by renewed safe-haven demand. The pair has been recovering from its late-January crash, when price collapsed from all-time highs above $121 to a low around $64 in early February, a drop of roughly 47%.

articles.title: AUD/USD jumps as hot CPI fuels RBA tightening bets
articles.snippet: The Australian Dollar (AUD) rallies over 0.80% against the US Dollar on Wednesday, courtesy of a red-hot inflation report, which increased speculation of further tightening by the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA).

articles.title: Gold reclaims $5,200 on trade jitters and Fed cut bets
articles.snippet: Gold prices rise over 1% on Wednesday amid uncertainty on US trade policies and expectations that the Federal Reserve (Fed) will ease policy through the rest of the year. At the time of writing, XAU/USD trades at $5,204 after bouncing off its worst levels at $5,121.

articles.title: Fed’s Schmid: Politics do not enter Fed policy debates
articles.snippet: Jeffrey Schmid, President of the Federal Reserve Bank of Kansas City, said on Wednesday that the Fed system has layers of independence and that politics do not enter Fed policy debates.

articles.title: USD/JPY advances as Japanese Yen falters on reduced BoJ tightening confidence
articles.snippet: USD/JPY trades around 156.30 on Wednesday at the time of writing, up 0.26% on the day, as the Japanese Yen (JPY) remains under pressure against the US Dollar (USD). The pair extends its recent upward move amid growing uncertainty over the interest rate path of the Bank of Japan (BoJ).

articles.title: NZD/USD climbs on weaker US Dollar following Trump’s State of Union speech
articles.snippet: NZD/USD trades around 0.5980 on Wednesday at the time of writing, up 0.27% on the day and extending its rebound for a second consecutive day. The pair benefits from a weaker US Dollar (USD) after US President Donald Trump delivered his State of the Union address before Congress.

articles.title: GBP/USD climbs as US Dollar stalls amid trade uncertainty
articles.snippet: The Pound Sterling (GBP) advances during the North American session on Wednesday as the Greenback remains unchanged amid uncertainty over US trade policies, with investors awaiting results of one of the seven megacap companies after the Wall Street close.

articles.title: Gold consolidates as investors weigh US tariffs, Iran talks and Fed outlook
articles.snippet: Gold (XAU/USD) stages a modest rebound on Wednesday, trimming part of the previous day’s losses as fresh uncertainty over US trade policy and lingering geopolitical tensions in the Middle East continue to underpin safe-haven demand.

articles.title: Silver Price Forecast: RSI recovers above 50, upside bias builds
articles.snippet: Silver (XAG/USD) gains traction on Wednesday after coming under modest pressure on the previous day, as lingering geopolitical and economic risks keep dip buyers active.

articles.title: GBP/USD: Rich valuation under rising BoE cut risk – HSBC
articles.snippet: HSBC Global Research argues that GBP/USD looks expensive versus rate differentials as markets increasingly price a more dovish Bank of England. Sterling remains pressured after a narrow February vote to hold policy.

articles.title: Silver rises sharply on safe-haven demand amid trade, geopolitical risks
articles.snippet: Silver (XAG/USD) accelerates on Wednesday and trades around $90.70 at the time of writing, up 3.90% on the day. The white metal benefits from an environment marked by rising trade and geopolitical uncertainties, which revives demand for safe-haven assets.

articles.title: US: Confidence improves as Fed stays cautious – DBS
articles.snippet: DBS Group Research highlights that Boston Fed President Susan Collins signalled US interest rates are likely to stay on hold, describing policy as mildly restrictive with inflation still above 2%. Futures have pushed the expected first rate cut to September.

articles.title: USD/CHF climbs as shifting Fed outlook supports the Greenback
articles.snippet: USD/CHF edges higher on Wednesday, drawing support from a firmer US Dollar, which is exerting pressure on the Swiss Franc (CHF). At the time of writing, the pair is trading around 0.7746, after bouncing off an intraday low of 0.7719.

articles.title: AUD/USD rises on robust inflation data, boosting RBA tightening bets
articles.snippet: AUD/USD trades around 0.7090 on Wednesday at the time of writing, up 0.42% on the day, supported by an unexpected acceleration in inflationary pressures in Australia, which revives expectations of a restrictive monetary policy stance.

articles.title: Trump digs in his heels on tariffs — with major implications for the U.S. dollar
articles.snippet: Market strategists weighed in on President Donald Trump’s State of the Union address late Tuesday and came away with some important implications for the U.S. dollar.

articles.title: Rupee declines 6 paise to settle at 90.95 against US dollar
articles.snippet: A sharp fall in domestic equity markets and uncertainties over the India-US trade deal further pressured the local unit, while foreign fund inflows lent some support, forex traders said

articles.title: Dogecoin (DOGE) Dips Into Red as Bearish Pressure Quietly Builds Today
articles.snippet: Dogecoin started a fresh decline below the $0.10 zone against the US Dollar. DOGE is now consolidating losses and might face hurdles near $0.0950 and $0.10. DOGE price started a fresh decline below the $0.10 level. The price is trading below the $0.0950 level…

articles.title: Rupee falls 7 paise to 90.96 against the US dollar in early trade
articles.snippet: Rupee drops 7 paise to 90.96 against the dollar amid rising crude prices and weak equity markets, but FII inflows provide support.

articles.title: Rupee falls 7 paise to 90.96 against the US dollar in early trade
articles.snippet: The Indian Rupee experienced a dip of 7 paise against the US Dollar, trading at 90.96 in early trade. This decline was influenced by escalating global crude oil prices and a strengthening dollar. Domestic equity markets also opened on a weak note, adding pres…

articles.title: Banks are testing a new type of crypto dollar called stablecoins. Here’s what that means for consumers
articles.snippet: OpenSea reports banks are testing stablecoins, digital tokens pegged to the USD, to enhance payment efficiency for consumers.

articles.title: 21shares Launches Spot Sui ETF (TSUI) in the United States
articles.snippet: U.S. spot ETF expands regulated access to the Sui ecosystem as institutional momentum accelerates U.S. spot ETF expands regulated access to the Sui ecosystem as institutional momentum accelerates

What did the rally do to the technicals?Israeli press: There is a high chance of an attack on IranFed's Musalem: Inflation is almost a full percentage point above targetU.S. Treasury sells $70 billion of 5 year notes at a high yield of 3.615%Bitcoin rips to $68,000 in a quick turnaroundEIA weekly US crude oil invent

articles.title: Forex Today: Australian Dollar surges to two-week highs after strong CPI data
articles.snippet: The Australian Dollar (AUD) skyrocketed to near a two-week high after a hotter-than-expected January inflation report, fueling speculation of additional rate hikes by the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA).

articles.title: USD/CNY: Watching fix as downside bias persists – OCBC
articles.snippet: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong note recent RMB strength as USD/CNY fell sharply after holidays, tracking a heavy USD/CNH. The move reflects a softer Dollar, better risk sentiment and perceived policy tolerance for RMB appreciation.

[0.50] XAG/USD surges above $90 as tariff fears temper and supply deficit fuels rally
XAG/USD surged about 4% on Tuesday, rallying sharply to close near $91 in a session driven by renewed safe-haven demand. The pair has been recovering from its late-January crash, when price collapsed from all-time highs above $121 to a low around $64 in early February, a drop of roughly 47%.
